data.speaker: narrator
data.text: Sorrel staggers, and all the meanness drains out of his stance at once, leaving something small and scared behind it.

data.speaker: Sorrel
data.text: How. How are you still standing. That is not. You should not be able to do that.

data.speaker: Sorrel
data.text: Fine. Fine! Sorrel knows when to fall back. Up the road I go, up to Brack, where it is safe, where you cannot follow without going through everything he has got.

data.speaker: Sorrel
data.text: You will not climb that hill, keeper. Nobody climbs that hill. Brack will see to it.

data.speaker: narrator
data.text: He gathers his leashes and bolts up the high road, calling a name ahead of him as he goes.
